skill.name: swiftui-expert-skill
skill.description: Build, review, and modernize SwiftUI code with guidance for state management, view composition, performance, navigation, text formatting, image handling, and iOS 26 Liquid Glass patterns.
skill.summary: SwiftUI guidance for modern APIs, state management, performance, navigation, and Liquid Glass adoption.
skill.author: avdlee

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, reconnaissance, network, and weak-crypto indicators, but review found they are SwiftUI documentation false positives. The skill contains markdown guidance and Swift examples, with no executable scripts, no command execution instructions, and no evidence of data exfiltration or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: False Positive Static Command Patterns
low_findings.description: The external command detections are caused by inline code formatting and Swift fenced examples, not Ruby backtick execution or shell commands. Representative locations show reference links, SwiftUI modifiers, and code samples rather than executable marketplace code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sampled locations are markdown guidance and Swift code fences. No shell command invocation, script file, or executable command path was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Patterns
low_findings.description: The high blocker and reconnaissance detections map to SwiftUI terms such as NavigationStack, AnyView, UIImage(data:), and UI sizing guidance. These are application UI examples, not cryptographic primitives or system discovery behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed contexts are SwiftUI examples about images, navigation, and view structure. I found no hashing, encryption, system probing, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Benign Documentation URL Example
low_findings.description: The single hardcoded URL is an Apple link inside a SwiftUI markdown Text example. It is not network code and does not send user data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears inside a string literal demonstrating markdown links in SwiftUI Text. There is no fetch, URLSession call, webhook, or credential handling at this location.

content.value_statement: SwiftUI projects can accumulate deprecated APIs, fragile state, and slow view updates. This skill gives focused guidance for modern, maintainable SwiftUI code.
actual_capabilities: Reviews SwiftUI state management and property wrapper choices.
Suggests modern replacements for deprecated SwiftUI APIs.
Guides view extraction, composition, and layout decisions.
Identifies performance risks in lists, body work, and state updates.
Provides patterns for sheets, navigation, scrolling, text formatting, and image optimization.
Explains iOS 26 Liquid Glass usage with availability fallbacks.
limitations: Does not run or compile Swift projects by itself.
Does not replace Apple documentation for final platform compatibility checks.
Focuses on SwiftUI guidance, not UIKit-heavy architecture decisions.
Liquid Glass guidance applies only when the target platform supports it.

use_cases.title: Review a SwiftUI Feature
use_cases.description: Find state, navigation, layout, and performance issues before merging a feature.

use_cases.title: Modernize Legacy Views
use_cases.description: Replace older SwiftUI APIs with current patterns while preserving behavior.

use_cases.title: Plan a New App Screen
use_cases.description: Design data flow, view structure, and platform fallbacks before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Review My View
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one SwiftUI file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this SwiftUI view for state management, deprecated APIs, accessibility, and simple performance issues. Explain the highest priority fixes first.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor for Modern SwiftUI
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this SwiftUI screen to use modern APIs. Preserve behavior, explain each change, and call out any iOS version requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Improve List Performance
prompt_templates.scenario: Diagnose slow scrolling or frequent updates.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this SwiftUI list for identity, filtering, state update, and body computation problems. Recommend focused changes that reduce redraw work.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Complex Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for navigation, sheets, and async work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a SwiftUI implementation plan for this flow. Include state ownership, navigation types, sheet presentation, async task handling, and fallback strategy.

output_examples.input: A SwiftUI profile screen that uses NavigationView, foregroundColor, and several local state variables.
output: A prioritized review of deprecated API usage.
State ownership recommendations for local and shared data.
A short migration plan to NavigationStack and foregroundStyle.

output_examples.input: A scrolling feed with inline filtering, image decoding, and unstable ForEach identity.
output: A performance diagnosis focused on identity and body work.
Recommendations to prefilter data and stabilize IDs.
Optional image downsampling guidance for large images.

output_examples.input: A request to add iOS 26 Liquid Glass to a toolbar.
output: A platform-gated approach using native glass effects.
Fallback guidance for earlier iOS versions.
Notes on where interactive glass is appropriate.

best_practices: Share the target iOS version and any required backward compatibility constraints.
Provide complete SwiftUI view context, including models and navigation state.
Ask for prioritized findings when reviewing large or complex screens.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to apply Liquid Glass unless the feature explicitly needs it.
Do not treat suggestions as compiled proof without running Xcode checks.
Do not provide isolated snippets when the issue depends on shared state or navigation context.

faq.question: Can this skill write new SwiftUI code?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help plan and draft SwiftUI views using modern APIs and platform-aware patterns.

faq.question: Does it support older iOS versions?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes legacy guidance and recommends availability checks when newer APIs are used.

faq.question: Can it review performance problems?
faq.answer: Yes. It focuses on view identity, state updates, body work, lists, and image handling.

faq.question: Does it enforce a specific architecture?
faq.answer: No. It encourages testable business logic without requiring MVVM, VIPER, or another named architecture.

faq.question: Can it compile or run my app?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and code suggestions, but build verification must happen in your development environment.

faq.question: When should I use the Liquid Glass guidance?
faq.answer: Use it only for iOS 26 or later designs, and include fallbacks for earlier supported versions.
